claim_bullets.bullet_text: A variable shadows an ancestor when names match, (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.47-47).
JavaScript searches environments from child to parent, (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.47-47).
A function is pure if its x is defined within its own environment, (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.47-47).

source_record_text: When a variable has the same name as an ancestor environment's binding, it is said to shadow the ancestor. JavaScript searches for a binding starting with the function's own environment and then each parent in turn until it finds one. An interesting thing happens when a variable has the same name as an ancestor environment's variable.
